<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:浮田国造]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:浮田国造]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:浮田国造]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan
| name        = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko
| crest       = 
| crestcaption = 
| honsei      = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}
| founder     = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}
| type        = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}}
| origin      = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}
| base        = Ukita Province<br /> (later {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}} {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Q16769301|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}})
| notable members = See [[#Notable Members]]
| branches    = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}<br />{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}<br />{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
}}

'''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}.

== Overview ==

=== Ancestry ===
According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}, appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, as the first ruler of the region.

=== Clan ===
The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|WD=Q1373049|lt=Jingō-keiun|1=Jingo-keiun}} (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公).

== Territory ==

=== Domain ===
The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Q16769301|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Sōma, Fukushima|en|Sōma, Fukushima|WD=Q696456|lt=Sōma, Fukushima|1=Sōma, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Minamisōma|en|Minamisōma|WD=Q642094|lt=Minamisōma|1=Minamisōma}}, and parts of {{ill|Shinchi, Fukushima|en|Shinchi, Fukushima|WD=Q1205416|lt=Shinchi, Fukushima|1=Shinchi, Fukushima}} and {{ill|Iitate, Fukushima|en|Iitate, Fukushima|WD=Q674500|lt=Iitate, Fukushima|1=Iitate, Fukushima}} in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Q85876227|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south.

During the reforms following the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|WD=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms|1=Taika Reform}}, the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.<ref>Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122.</ref>

== Religious Significance ==

=== Clan Shrine ===
The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko.

=== Kofuns ===

* '''{{ill|Sakurai Kofun|en|Sakurai Kofun|WD=Q11537784|lt=Sakurai Kofun|1=Sakurai Kofun}}'''
: Located in {{ill|Minamisōma|en|Minamisōma|WD=Q642094|lt=Minamisōma|1=Minamisōma}}, Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016.</ref>

* '''{{ill|Mano Kofun Cluster|en|Mano Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11583561|lt=Mano Kofungun|1=Mano Kofun Cluster}}'''
: Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* [[Fushū]]
* {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}}



[[ja:浮田国造]]

<!--Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko-->
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->


{{wikidata link|Q108487214}}
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[Category:Mutsu Province]]
[[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
[[Category:Page1]]

{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 =
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 = {{ill|Kimikobe clan|ja|吉弥侯部|lt=Kimikobe clan|lt_ja=吉弥侯部氏}}
|家祖 = {{ill|Kaga-Wake|ja|鹿我別|lt=Kaga-Wake-no-Ōkimi|lt_ja=賀我別王}}
|種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}}
|出身地 = {{ill|Ōyamato Province|ja|大和国|lt=Ōyamato Province|lt_ja=大倭国}}
|根拠地 = Ukita Province<br />(later {{ill|Uda District, Mutsu|ja|宇多郡|lt=Uda District|lt_ja=宇多郡}}, {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|lt=Namekata District|lt_ja=行方郡}} of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}})
|人物 = See [[#People|People]]
|支流 = {{ill|Harima-Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}}<br />{{ill|Kamitsukenu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}}<br />{{ill|Shimotsukenu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}
}}
'''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''ukita no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''ukita kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled [[#Territory|Ukita Province]] (eastern {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}).

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry ===
* According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|lt=Emperor Seimu|lt_ja=成務朝}}, '''{{ill|Kaga-Wake|ja|鹿我別|lt=Kaga-Wake-no-Ōkimi|lt_ja=賀我別王}}''', 5th-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed kuni no miyatsuko.

=== Clan ===
'''{{ill|Kimikobe clan|ja|吉弥侯部|lt=Kimikobe clan|lt_ja=吉弥侯部氏}}''' (''kimikobe-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} unknown), kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukenu clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukenu clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, and {{ill|Harima-Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}}. On the [[July 19|19th day of the 7th month]] of {{ill|Jingo-keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 1 ([[767]]), Kimikobe-no-Ishimaro, a Ge-shō-rokui-jō, Kun-jūtō of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡}}, was granted the surname '''Kamitsukenu-Mutsu-{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)|lt=Kimi|lt_ja=公}}'''.

== Base ==
=== Territory ===
The territory of the kuni no miyatsuko was the region then called '''Ukita Province''', corresponding to the later {{ill|ritsuryō province|ja|律令国}} of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}} '''{{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡}}''' and '''{{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|lt=Namekata District|lt_ja=行方郡}}''', equivalent to present-day {{ill|Sōma, Fukushima|ja|相馬市|lt=Sōma City|lt_ja=相馬市}}, {{ill|Minamisōma, Fukushima|ja|南相馬市|lt=Minamisōma City|lt_ja=南相馬市}}, {{ill|Shinchi, Fukushima|ja|新地町|lt=Shinchi Town|lt_ja=新地町}} in {{ill|Sōma District, Fukushima|ja|相馬郡|lt=Sōma District|lt_ja=相馬郡}}, and {{ill|Iitate, Fukushima|ja|飯舘村|lt=Iitate Village|lt_ja=飯舘村}} in the same district. Contemporary with the establishment of the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko, the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} ({{ill|Igu District|ja|伊具郡}}) was placed to the northwest, and the {{ill|Somewa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} ({{ill|Shineha District|ja|標葉郡}}) to the south.

After the {{ill|Taika Reform|ja|大化の改新}}, when the {{ill|hyō system|ja|評|lt=hyō system|lt_ja=評制}} was implemented nationwide, there is a theory that the {{ill|Uda River|ja|宇多川}} basin became Uda-hyō, and the {{ill|Nitta River|ja|新田川}} basin became Namekata-hyō<ref>{{ill|Hirakawa Minami|ja|平川南}}, ''Tōhoku "Kaidō" no Kodaishi'', {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 2012, p. 122.</ref>.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
'''{{ill|Komi-ne Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社}}''', the sole {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡}} and a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}.

=== Tombs ===
*'''{{ill|Sakurai Kofun|ja|桜井古墳}}''' (''sakurai-kofun'')
*:A 74 m long {{ill|keyhole-rectangle kofun|ja|前方後方墳}} in {{ill|Minamisōma, Fukushima|ja|南相馬市|lt=Minamisōma City|lt_ja=南相馬市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, constructed in the late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} to the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Based on its location and construction date, there is a theory that it is the tomb of {{ill|Kaga-Wake-no-Mikoto|ja|鹿我別|lt=Kaga-Wake-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=鹿我別命}}, the first ancestor of the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm The Genealogy of the Madaramoku Clan of the Kimikobe Kabane]," ''Koju Kinobōma'', 2016.</ref>.
*'''{{ill|Mano Kofun Group|ja|真野古墳群}}''' (''mano-kofun-gun'')
*:A large-scale late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} in Minamisōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, composed of 2 {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} and numerous {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}}.

== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}

== See also ==
*{{ill|Fushū|ja|俘囚}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:うきたのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:陸奥国]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:浮田国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
[[Category:Page2]]
[[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]
